title: Set 1.3 - CA Hierarchy, CRL/OCSP, Certificates, OAuth/FIDO & TLS Email
subject: PKI
topic: CA Hierarchy, CRL/OCSP, Certificates, OAuth/FIDO & TLS Email

questions.question: What is the significance of a Cross-Certificate in a CA hierarchy?
options: It links two different CAs in a trust relationship
It is used for encryption purposes
It is the top-level certificate in a hierarchy
questions.description: Correct answer: It links two different CAs in a trust relationship.

questions.question: Can a CA's private key be disclosed to the public?
options: No, it should be kept confidential
Only during the certificate renewal process
Only if the CA is audited regularly
questions.description: Correct answer: No, it should be kept confidential.

questions.question: In a Hierarchical CA Model, what is the role of an Intermediate CA?
options: To issue digital certificates directly to end entities
To bridge the gap between the Root CA and end entities
To manage the public key infrastructure
questions.description: Correct answer: To bridge the gap between the Root CA and end entities.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a Certificate Revocation List (CRL)?
questions.description: Correct answer: To list revoked certificates.
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: What does OCSP stand for in the context of certificates?
questions.description: Correct answer: Online Certificate Status Protocol.
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: Which of the following is a real-time method for checking the status of a digital certificate?
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: How does OCSP differ from CRL in terms of certificate status checking?
options: OCSP is offline, while CRL is real-time
CRL is offline, while OCSP is real-time
questions.description: Correct answer: CRL is offline, while OCSP is real-time.
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of certificate revocation?
options: To maintain the secrecy of certificates
To invalidate compromised or untrusted certificates
questions.description: Correct answer: To invalidate compromised or untrusted certificates.
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: Which protocol provides a more scalable and efficient solution for checking certificate revocation status?
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: In a PKI infrastructure, who typically issues Certificate Revocation Lists?
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: What is the consequence of relying solely on CRL for certificate revocation checking?
options: Potential delays in identifying revoked certificates
questions.description: Correct answer: Potential delays in identifying revoked certificates.
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: Which extension is commonly found in X.509 certificates and contains information about CRL distribution points?
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: What role does OCSP Stapling play in certificate revocation checking?
options: It allows multiple CAs to issue certificates
It reduces the load on the OCSP responder
It encrypts the CRL during transmission
It signs the CRL for authentication
questions.description: Correct answer: It reduces the load on the OCSP responder.
tags: Certificate Revocation Lists (CRL) and Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)

questions.question: Which type of certificate is used to secure communication between a user's browser and a website?
What is the primary purpose of a Code Signing Certificate?
Which class of certificate requires more rigorous validation of the certificate holder's identity?
What is the primary use of a Client Certificate?
questions.description: Correct answer: Authenticating users to a website.

questions.question: Which type of certificate is commonly used for securing email communication?
What is the difference between a Wildcard Certificate and a SAN Certificate?
options: Wildcard Certificates are for code signing, while SAN Certificates are for email encryption
SAN Certificates support multiple domain names, while Wildcard Certificates cover only one domain and its subdomains
Wildcard Certificates require more rigorous validation
SAN Certificates are only used for server authentication
questions.description: Correct answer: SAN Certificates support multiple domain names, while Wildcard Certificates cover only one domain and its subdomains.

questions.question: Which type of certificate is commonly used for securing software applications and updates?
In a public key infrastructure (PKI), what is the role of a Root Certificate?
options: Establishing trust by signing other certificates
questions.description: Correct answer: Establishing trust by signing other certificates.

questions.question: Which class of certificate may be issued without verifying the identity of the certificate holder?
What is the primary purpose of an Intermediate Certificate?
options: Verifying the identity of the Root CA

questions.question: What does FIPS stand for in FIPS 140-2?
questions.description: Correct answer: Federal Information Processing Standard.

questions.question: What is the purpose of FIPS 140-2 certification in the context of cryptographic modules?
options: Verifying the security of cryptographic algorithms and modules
questions.description: Correct answer: Verifying the security of cryptographic algorithms and modules.

questions.question: Which organization is responsible for issuing FIPS 140-2 certifications?
options: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE)
questions.description: Correct answer: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST).

questions.question: What is the highest level of security assurance provided by FIPS 140-2?
Which of the following is a requirement for FIPS 140-2 Level 2 certification?
In FIPS 140-2, what does the term "module" refer to?
questions.description: Correct answer: A unit of hardware.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a requirement for FIPS 140-2 Level 1 certification?
What role does the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP) play in FIPS 140-2 certification?
Which cryptographic algorithm is commonly used in FIPS 140-2 compliant modules for data encryption?
Why is FIPS 140-2 certification important for organizations using cryptographic modules?
options: It verifies the security of cryptographic algorithms and modules
questions.description: Correct answer: It verifies the security of cryptographic algorithms and modules.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of OpenID?
questions.description: Correct answer: Authenticating users across websites.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common use case for OAuth?
options: Authenticating users with a single sign-on (SSO)
questions.description: Correct answer: Authenticating users with a single sign-on (SSO).

questions.question: What role does the Authorization Server play in the OAuth 2.0 framework?
In OAuth, what is the purpose of an access token?
options: Verifying the identity of the client application

questions.question: Which OAuth 2.0 grant type is suitable for client applications running on a web server?
What does OAuth 2.0's Implicit Grant flow primarily focus on?
Which role does the Resource Owner play in OAuth 2.0?
options: The user seeking access to a protected resource
The authorization server issuing refresh tokens
questions.description: Correct answer: The user seeking access to a protected resource.

questions.question: In the context of OpenID Connect, what does ID Token represent?
Which OAuth 2.0 grant type is designed for non-interactive clients?
What is the main advantage of using OAuth for API access control?
options: It provides end-to-end encryption for data transmission
It simplifies user authentication across multiple applications
It allows secure storage of user credentials
It enables third-party applications to access resources without exposing user credentials
questions.description: Correct answer: It enables third-party applications to access resources without exposing user credentials.

questions.question: What is the purpose of an authentication protocol?
Which authentication factor involves something you know?
Which authentication factor involves something you have?
What is two-factor authentication (2FA)?
questions.description: Correct answer: Combining two authentication factors.

questions.question: Which of the following is a common challenge in biometric authentication?
questions.description: Correct answer: All of the above.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a CAPTCHA in authentication?
Which authentication method is susceptible to phishing attacks?
What is the role of a nonce in authentication?
Which authentication protocol is commonly used for secure email communication?
What is the purpose of a time-based one-time password (TOTP) in authentication?
questions.description: Correct answer: Add a time-sensitive element.

questions.question: What does FIDO stand for in FIDO authentication?
Which key elements are used in FIDO authentication?
What is the purpose of FIDO U2F (Universal 2nd Factor)?
options: Authenticate users using a single factor
Provide a second factor for authentication
questions.description: Correct answer: Provide a second factor for authentication.

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of FIDO authentication?
questions.description: Correct answer: All of the above.

questions.question: Which protocol is commonly associated with FIDO authentication?
How does FIDO authentication enhance security compared to traditional passwords?
What is a FIDO Authenticator?
questions.description: Correct answer: Hardware device for authentication.

questions.question: Which industry is FIDO authentication commonly used in?
questions.description: Correct answer: All of the above.

questions.question: What is the role of a relying party in FIDO authentication?
questions.description: Correct answer: Provide services to the user.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a FIDO authentication protocol?
What does SSL stand for in the context of secure communication?
Which layer of the OSI model does SSL/TLS operate at?
What is the primary purpose of SSL/TLS?
questions.description: Correct answer: All of the above.

questions.question: Which protocol is the successor to SSL?
What is a certificate authority (CA) in the context of SSL/TLS?
options: An entity that issues digital certificates
questions.description: Correct answer: An entity that issues digital certificates.

questions.question: Which port is commonly used for secure HTTPS communication?
What is a self-signed certificate in SSL/TLS?
options: A certificate without a private key
A certificate signed by the server itself
A certificate used for client authentication only
questions.description: Correct answer: A certificate signed by the server itself.

questions.question: Which key exchange method is commonly used in SSL/TLS protocols?
What is the purpose of the TLS handshake process?
questions.description: Correct answer: All of the above.

questions.question: Which version of SSL/TLS is considered insecure and should be avoided?
What is the main purpose of PGP (Pretty Good Privacy)?
Who developed PGP?
What type of encryption does PGP primarily use?
questions.description: Correct answer: Both A and B.

questions.question: What is a public keyring in PGP used for?
Which part of the PGP key pair is kept secret?
What is the purpose of a PGP key fingerprint?
options: Verify the integrity of a key
questions.description: Correct answer: Verify the integrity of a key.

questions.question: Which standard is commonly used for securing email communication with digital signatures and encryption?
questions.description: Correct answer: S/MIME (Secure/Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions).

questions.question: What is the role of a certificate authority in S/MIME?
Which encryption algorithm is commonly used in S/MIME for securing email content?
What is the purpose of an S/MIME certificate in email communication?
questions.description: Correct answer: All of the above.
